title: CPU Runaway — Python Worker
description: A tight loop in a background task worker consumed 94% CPU for 8 minutes before the investigation engine identified and isolated the process.

alert.detail: CPU utilization exceeded 95% for 5 consecutive minutes

evidence_sources_used.detail: 3 errors — queue depth 847, worker pool saturated

hypotheses.cause: Runaway python3 worker in tight CPU loop
GC thrash caused by object accumulation

root_cause.summary: A python3 worker process (pid 4421) entered a tight loop due to an unhandled exception in the request queue consumer, consuming 94.2% CPU and saturating the thread pool. No circuit breaker was configured for queue depth overflow.
supporting_evidence: python3 pid 4421 at 94.2% CPU for >5 minutes
Worker thread pool saturated — requests dropped
reasoning_steps: CPU 97.4% with single process at 94.2% → likely single runaway process
process_tree shows python3 pid 4421 with no children — not a fork bomb
Log shows queue depth 847 — worker not draining queue, confirms tight loop
PSI pressure avg60=72.11 consistent with sustained single-core saturation
GC pause 1240ms indicates tight object allocation loop, not I/O wait

outcome.resolution_note: CPU dropped to 4.2% within 90 seconds of process kill. Root cause confirmed — exception handler missing in queue consumer.
